perturbation.original_text: 1.14 "Specifications" shall mean the Flix Software customizations and integrations described on Exhibit B.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.14 "Specifications" shall mean the *initial* Flix Software customizations and integrations described on Exhibit B.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of the word 'initial' to the definition of 'Specifications' in Section 1.14 creates ambiguity. While Exhibit B outlines the initial customizations, later modifications or updates could be argued as outside the original 'Specifications'. This contradicts Section 3.1 which requires deliverables to substantially conform to the 'relevant Specification' without explicitly stating if it includes updates after the initial delivery. This change introduces uncertainty around whether subsequent versions, alterations, or updates fall under the initial specifications, potentially undermining Wildform's responsibilities if the specifications are interpreted in a restrictive manner.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Deliverables. Subject to payment by On2 of the Closing Date Payment Amount in accordance with the Asset Purchase Agreement, Wildform shall deliver each Deliverable in accordance with the timeframe (the "Timeframe") set forth in Exhibit B, which Exhibit is made a part of this Agreement. On2 will use commercially reasonable efforts to cooperate with Wildform as necessary in completing the Development Work and delivery of the Deliverables. Upon receipt of each Deliverable, On2 shall have a period of thirty (30) working days within which to inspect such Deliverable based upon the relevant Specification (an "Acceptance Period"). Should On2 find that any Deliverable does not substantially conform to the relevant Specification, On2 shall promptly so notify Wildform in writing, which notice shall provide sufficiently detailed explanation of the non-conformities so as to allow Wildform to reproduce them (any such notice is referred to herein as a "Nonconformance Notice"). Each of the Deliverables shall be deemed accepted by On2 unless On2 delivers to Wildform a Nonconformance Notice within thirty (30) business days of the delivery of such Deliverable. In the event that Wildform receives such Nonconformance Notice from On2, Wildform shall make commercially reasonable efforts to modify such Deliverable and re-deliver it to On2 after any such modification is completed. Each Deliverable shall be deemed to have been accepted by On2 when either (i) On2 notifies Wildform in writing of its acceptance of such Deliverables or (ii) On2 does not deliver a Nonconformance Notice to Wildform within the Acceptance Period. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, On2 shall not unreasonably withhold acceptance of any Deliverable.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'initial' to the definition of 'Specifications' in Section 1.14 introduces a strong in-text contradiction with Section 3.1. Section 3.1 requires deliverables to conform to the 'relevant Specification'. By limiting the 'Specifications' to 'initial' customizations, it creates uncertainty about whether subsequent updates or modifications are included. This directly impacts Wildform's obligation to modify and re-deliver deliverables in response to a Nonconformance Notice from On2. If the specifications are interpreted narrowly as 'initial' only, Wildform could argue that later versions are outside the original specifications, potentially undermining their responsibilities under Section 3.1, therefore, a strong in-text contradiction has been created.

perturbation.original_text: Wildform shall perform the Transition Services for which it is responsible hereunder following commonly accepted standards of care in the industry and exercising the same degree of care as it exercises in performing the same or similar services for its own business as of the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Wildform shall perform the Transition Services for which it is responsible hereunder following commonly accepted standards of care *that Wildform deems acceptable* in the industry and exercising the same degree of care as it exercises in performing the same or similar services for its own business as of the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'that Wildform deems acceptable', the standard of care becomes subjective, based on Wildform's assessment. This creates a contradiction with Article VI, specifically 6.3 which states 'EXCEPT AS PROVIDED IN SUBSECTIONS 6.1 AND 6.2 ABOVE, THE DELIVERABLES AND INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY ARE PROVIDED 'AS-IS' WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND', because it allows Wildform to argue that any defects or failures are acceptable under their subjective standard, even if industry standards suggest otherwise, essentially nullifying implied standards in the 'AS-IS' clause. This introduced ambiguity may cause potential legal disputes over the actual standard of service required and provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3 No Warranty. EXCEPT AS PROVIDED IN SUBSECTIONS 6.1 AND 6.2 ABOVE, THE DELIVERABLES AND INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY ARE PROVIDED "AS-IS" WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND AND BOTH PARTIES EXPRESSLY AGREE AND ACKNOWLEDGE THAT THE DELIVERABLES AND INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY ARE BEING PROVIDED WITHOUT ANY REPRESENTATIONS, WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, STATUTORY, ARISING OUT OF A COURSE OF DEALING OR USAGE OF TRADE OR OTHERWISE INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF MERCHANTABILITY, MERCHANTABLE QUALITY, FITNESS OR ADEQUACY FOR ANY PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR USE, QUALITY, PRODUCTIVENESS, CAPACITY, OR THAT THE OPERATION OF THE DELIVERABLES OR INCORPORTED TECHNOLOGY WILL BE ERROR-FREE. NO ORAL OR WRITTEN INFORMATION OR ADVICE GIVEN BY A PARTY, ITS DIRECTORS, OFFICERS, EMPLOYEES, LICENSORS, SUPPLIERS, AGENTS, OR TO ANYONE ELSE WHO HAS BEEN INVOLVED IN THE CREATION, PRODUCTION, LICENSING, SUBLICENSING, SUPPLY OR DELIVERY OF THE DELIVERABLES OR INCORPORATED TECHNOLOGY WILL CREATE A REPRESENTATION, CONDITION, OR WARRANTY AND NEITHER PARTY MAY NOT RELY ON SUCH INFORMATION OR ADVICE.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'that Wildform deems acceptable' introduces a subjective standard of care, which directly contradicts the 'AS-IS' warranty disclaimer in Section 6.3. The original text mandates adherence to 'commonly accepted standards of care in the industry,' providing an objective benchmark. However, the modified text allows Wildform to define what it considers acceptable, potentially undermining the expectations created by the 'AS-IS' clause. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the standard of care is now open to interpretation by Wildform, which means, in effect, there is no industry standard to be held to because Wildform subjectively determines it, which negates any possible interpretations of liability or standards that may be referenced in the 'AS-IS' warranty. This is a structural flaw that creates uncertainty about Wildform's obligations, and thus a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During the first twelve (12) months of the Term, Wildform will provide On2 with reasonable technical support for the Deliverables via phone and email during Wildform's normal business hours with respect to the following: o Train On2 personnel in the way the code works. o Train On2 personnel in how the software is designed, maintained and compiled. o Provide ongoing support during the 12-month term for On2's further development and integration.
perturbation.changed_text: During the first twelve (12) months of the Term, Wildform will provide On2 with reasonable technical support *if reasonably available* for the Deliverables via phone and email during Wildform's normal business hours with respect to the following: o Train On2 personnel in the way the code works. o Train On2 personnel in how the software is designed, maintained and compiled. o Provide ongoing support during the 12-month term for On2's further development and integration.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'if reasonably available' introduces ambiguity into Wildform's obligation to provide technical support. This contradicts the intent of Section 2.1 which states 'Wildform will provide to On2 the services indicated on such Exhibit (the "Transition Services") during the time period for each such Transition Service set forth in such Exhibit'. Now Wildform can refuse support if it deems support is not 'reasonably available'. This ambiguity creates a potential conflict, regarding whether Wildform is truly obligated to support On2 and to which extent. The initial text implies an obligation to provide technical support, while the added qualifier allows Wildform to potentially avoid or reduce support, undermining the entire Section 2.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Transition Services. Upon the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in this Agreement, with respect to each of those services set forth on Exhibit A hereto, which Exhibit is made a part of this Agreement, Wildform will provide to On2 the services indicated on such Exhibit (the "Transition Services") during the time period for each such Transition Service set forth in such Exhibit (hereinafter referred to as the "Time Periods" for all of the Transition Services, and the "Time Period" for each Transition Service).
perturbation.justification: The original text states Wildform 'will provide' technical support, indicating a firm obligation. The changed text introduces 'if reasonably available,' which creates ambiguity and allows Wildform to potentially avoid providing support. This directly contradicts the 'will provide' language in Section 2.1, weakening Wildform's obligation to deliver the Transition Services as outlined in Exhibit A and making it unenforceable.
